primary_accounting_issue: Possibilité d’appliquer la comptabilité de couverture en comptes consolidés à un risque de change lié à des dividendes intragroupe comptabilisés en créance à recevoir

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge accounting eligibility questions, so IFRS 9 governs the identification of hedge accounting models and hedged items, displacing IAS 39 on the same issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low: IFRS 9 clearly supersedes IAS 39 for hedge accounting, while IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 remain relevant only to clarify foreign currency effects and net investment hedge constraints rather than replace IFRS 9.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the eligible hedged items and hedge accounting models in consolidated financial statements, including intragroup foreign currency exposures.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify when intragroup foreign currency monetary items create exchange differences that are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
This Interpretation constrains and clarifies the separate top-level model of hedging a net investment in a foreign operation.

treatment_families.family: General hedge accounting models available for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements
Net investment hedge model for foreign operations
Intragroup foreign currency exposures that can survive consolidation and therefore enter hedge model analysis

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: It is a recognized top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on recognized items when the exposure is eligible in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: It is a recognized top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on eligible forecast cash flows or similar exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: It is a distinct IFRS hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure on a net investment in a foreign operation, separately clarified by IFRIC 16.
